topic: Minimax lower bound for the interior continuous-treatment dose-response curve E[mu(t,X)] under anisotropic Holder smoothness (treatment-direction alpha, treatment-density beta, covariate-direction s), matching the published HOIF upper rate of Bonvini-Kennedy (arXiv:2207.11825); distinguished from incremental-effect/edge-dose continuous-exposure minimax bounds.

open_problems.open_problem: Prove a sharp minimax lower bound for the interior fixed-dose value theta(t)=E[mu(t,X)] over anisotropic Holder classes that matches the Bonvini-Kennedy higher-order influence-function upper rate.

source_refs.claim: They construct global, DR-learner, and mth-order HOIF estimators for continuous-treatment dose-response estimation and explicitly say a tight minimax lower bound remains elusive.
source_refs.author: Bonvini and Kennedy

source_refs.claim: They develop higher-order influence-function U-statistic estimators and rate-optimal non-root-n estimation for nonlinear functionals in nonparametric models.
source_refs.author: Robins, Li, Tchetgen Tchetgen, and van der Vaart
source_refs.venue: Probability and Statistics: Essays in Honor of David A. Freedman

source_refs.claim: They use localized fuzzy hypotheses to prove minimax lower bounds for a causal regression-functional hybrid, but for CATE rather than continuous-dose partial means.
source_refs.author: Kennedy, Balakrishnan, Robins, and Wasserman

open_problems.why_unsolved: The converse must perturb both the outcome regression and the conditional treatment density while preserving positivity, treatment-density normalization in the dose coordinate, the marginal law constraints used by the dose-response representation, and separate Holder smoothness in treatment and covariate directions. Standard one-dimensional regression bumps only recover the oracle smoothing difficulty, while existing nonlinear-functional lower bounds do not directly encode the fixed interior-dose localization and the HOIF nuisance-product bias scale.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A minimax lower-bound theorem for inf_hat_theta sup_{P in P(alpha,beta,s)} E_P[(hat_theta(t)-theta_P(t))^2] whose rate matches the Bonvini-Kennedy HOIF upper bound for an interior dose t, up to constants or explicitly stated logarithmic factors.

writing_exemplar.why: It is the paper that poses the exact optimality gap and separates dose smoothness, conditional-density smoothness, and covariate smoothness in the setup.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: continuous doses make fixed-dose effects non-root-n -> HOIF estimators improve the nuisance-product rate under Holder structure -> the matching minimax converse for the interior fixed-dose curve is still missing

method_exemplars.technique: Localized fuzzy-hypothesis lower-bound construction that combines nonparametric-regression and nonlinear-functional channels, with random-sign bumps calibrated by Hellinger or chi-square distance.
method_exemplars.why_closest: CATE is also a causal regression-functional hybrid, so its lower-bound decomposition is the nearest published template for proving that no estimator beats the HOIF-scale nuisance interaction.

method_exemplars.technique: Higher-order von Mises and U-statistic expansion with explicit projection approximation and bias-variance balancing.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The lower bound has to match the same HOIF remainder scale, so the converse should be calibrated against the estimator algebra used to produce the upper rate.

open_problems.open_problem: Determine the complete anisotropic phase diagram for pointwise and integrated interior dose-response risk, including the transition boundaries where oracle regression, HOIF nuisance products, or conditional-density estimation dominates.

source_refs.claim: They frame theta(t) as a regression-functional hybrid and distinguish smoothness of the dose-response curve, outcome regression, and conditional treatment density across dose and covariate directions.
source_refs.author: Bonvini and Kennedy

source_refs.claim: They give kernel-localized doubly robust DML inference for continuous-treatment dose-response and partial effects under high-level nuisance conditions, without a sharp minimax converse.
source_refs.author: Colangelo and Lee

source_refs.claim: They estimate conditional causal and structural functions with orthogonal signals and series projection, giving upper-bound inference machinery rather than a continuous-dose minimax phase diagram.
source_refs.author: Semenova and Chernozhukov

open_problems.why_unsolved: Published upper bounds are expressed as oracle terms plus procedure-specific nuisance remainders, while a rate frontier has to rule out estimators that exploit cancellations among mu, pi, and the marginal treatment density. The hard part is proving all elbows in alpha, beta, s, and covariate dimension under one law class rather than proving isolated lower bounds for a single smoothness ordering.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A sharp phase-diagram theorem listing the minimax pointwise and integrated squared-risk rates over the anisotropic Holder scale, with explicit transition inequalities among alpha, beta, s, and dimension.

writing_exemplar.why: Its setup already presents the estimator comparison and smoothness decomposition that a phase diagram would complete.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: DR learners reduce dose-response estimation to oracle regression plus nuisance remainders -> HOIF changes the nuisance-product frontier -> a full smoothness phase diagram must say which obstruction is fundamental in each regime

method_exemplars.technique: Separate lower-bound submodels for oracle smoothing and nuisance-function interaction, then take the maximum and prove the transition elbows.
method_exemplars.why_closest: Their CATE result exhibits an elbow and interpolation between regression and functional estimation, which is structurally analogous to the desired dose-response phase diagram.

method_exemplars.technique: Kernel-localized orthogonal score with cross-fitting and high-level nuisance conditions for asymptotic normality at nonparametric rates.
method_exemplars.why_closest: It supplies the first-order continuous-treatment inference benchmark whose rate conditions should appear as one face of the complete frontier.

open_problems.open_problem: Prove a formal separation between minimax lower bounds for incremental continuous-exposure effects or edge-dose limits and the unresolved interior fixed-dose dose-response minimax lower bound.

source_refs.claim: They derive minimax lower bounds for incremental continuous-exposure effects where effective sample size scales like n/delta and study the delta-to-infinity edge-dose regime.
source_refs.author: Schindl, Shen, and Kennedy

source_refs.claim: They target the standard fixed-dose response theta(t)=E[E(Y|A=t,X)] under positivity for interior t, not a stochastic tilt estimand or a boundary-dose limit.
source_refs.author: Bonvini and Kennedy

open_problems.why_unsolved: Incremental estimands smooth over the observed exposure law and depend on the tilt parameter delta, while the interior fixed-dose estimand localizes at a single treatment value and pays a different kernel/localization and conditional-density estimation cost. Existing incremental lower bounds therefore do not automatically imply a converse for the anisotropic partial mean at a fixed interior dose, but the exact non-reduction has not been formalized.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A separation theorem or counterexample showing two Holder submodels with the same incremental or edge-dose difficulty but different interior theta(t) minimax rates, or an experiment-comparison theorem proving non-reducibility between the estimands.

writing_exemplar.why: It is the closest paper with actual continuous-exposure minimax lower bounds, making it the natural foil for distinguishing the interior fixed-dose problem.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: standard dose-response needs positivity -> incremental tilts avoid this by changing the estimand -> those lower bounds do not resolve the interior fixed-dose minimax converse

method_exemplars.technique: Effective-sample-size lower-bound calibration for tilted exposure interventions, tracking how risk depends on the localization or tilt parameter.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The proof already handles continuous-exposure minimax lower bounds; a separation result would reuse its experiment while altering the target to fixed interior theta(t).

method_exemplars.technique: Interior fixed-dose pseudo-outcome and HOIF representation, used to define the target experiment that must be shown distinct from tilted or edge-dose experiments.
method_exemplars.why_closest: It gives the exact estimand and nuisance geometry whose local information should differ from incremental-effect lower-bound geometry.

open_problems.open_problem: Establish whether adaptive honest confidence intervals or bands for the interior dose-response curve can attain the sharp minimax rate when the anisotropic smoothness vector (alpha,beta,s) is unknown.

source_refs.claim: They study data-adaptive smoothing for nonregular parameters, including causal dose-response inference, and construct nearly optimal adaptive confidence intervals under conditions.
source_refs.author: Bibaut and van der Laan

source_refs.claim: They emphasize that nonparametric continuous-treatment dose-response curves are non-pathwise differentiable and study HAL-based plug-in estimation and empirical inference.
source_refs.author: Shi, Zhang, Hubbard, and van der Laan

source_refs.claim: They provide fast smoothness-dependent upper rates for continuous-dose estimators but do not settle the lower bound or adaptive honest inference frontier.
source_refs.author: Bonvini and Kennedy

open_problems.why_unsolved: Even with a sharp minimax rate, honest inference has to control bias from smoothing, projection, and HOIF truncation while the nuisance smoothness and conditional-density regularity are unknown. Adaptive confidence procedures for nonregular nonparametric targets often require self-similarity, undersmoothing, or an unavoidable excess radius, and the continuous-treatment conditional-density nuisance introduces additional anisotropic bias terms.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: An adaptive confidence-interval or confidence-band theorem with honest coverage over a specified anisotropic Holder scale, paired with a lower bound identifying any unavoidable adaptation penalty.

writing_exemplar.why: It frames dose-response inference as adaptive smoothing for nonregular causal parameters, which is the natural inferential arc after the rate frontier is fixed.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: fixed-dose dose-response is nonregular -> smoothing creates pathwise differentiable approximations -> unknown anisotropic smoothness raises the question of honest adaptive minimax inference

method_exemplars.technique: Data-adaptive smoothing selection with asymptotic normality for a pathwise-differentiable approximation and explicit bias-variance accounting.
method_exemplars.why_closest: It directly targets adaptive confidence intervals for nonregular causal dose-response-style parameters.

method_exemplars.technique: HOIF bias correction and order selection for fast dose-response estimation under smoothness classes.
method_exemplars.why_closest: An adaptive interval must either build on or prove limits relative to the same HOIF estimator family whose upper rate motivates the lower-bound problem.

literature_map:
Bonvini and Kennedy (2022, arXiv:2207.11825) is the anchor: it gives fast upper rates for continuous-treatment dose-response estimation and explicitly leaves a tight minimax lower bound open.
Kennedy, Ma, McHugh, and Small's earlier continuous-treatment DR pseudo-outcome work motivates the oracle-regression-plus-remainder decomposition used by later estimators.
Colangelo and Lee (2020, arXiv:2004.03036) give kernel-localized continuous-treatment DML inference under high-level nuisance conditions, but not a sharp rate converse.
Semenova and Chernozhukov (2017, arXiv:1702.06240) develop orthogonal-signal projection methods for conditional causal functions, useful as an upper/inference comparator.
Robins, Li, Tchetgen Tchetgen, and van der Vaart (2008/2016) supply the higher-order influence-function and nonlinear-functional minimax toolkit behind the Bonvini-Kennedy estimator.
Kennedy, Balakrishnan, Robins, and Wasserman (2022, arXiv:2203.00837) prove minimax CATE rates via localized fuzzy hypotheses; it is the closest lower-bound proof template but for a different causal hybrid target.
The main technical tension is that theta(t) is simultaneously a one-dimensional local regression target in t and a nonlinear functional of mu and pi.
A converse must preserve conditional-density normalization, positivity, and anisotropic Holder regularity while separating the estimand at the HOIF rate.
Schindl, Shen, and Kennedy (2024, arXiv:2409.11967) provide continuous-exposure minimax lower bounds for incremental effects and edge-dose limits, but those estimands differ from the interior fixed-dose curve.
Bibaut and van der Laan (2017, arXiv:1706.07408) and Shi et al. (2024, arXiv:2406.05607) show the inference-side difficulty: dose-response is nonregular and smoothing/adaptation choices are central.
The appended study-pipeline gaps were LATE, Manski bounds, and interval-regression questions; they are useful elsewhere but did not satisfy this run's dose-response minimax topic gate.
prior_proposal_map:
Inspected doc/formalization/research/stat_dose_response_minimax/state.json and pipeline.jsonl: the run is initialized but has no prior proposal, reviewer JSON, or rejected angle for this qid.
Track A inspected adjacent Stat entry stat_ate_overlap_decay and found minimax-converse/HOIF-adjacent notes, but the target is overlap-tail ATE rather than continuous-dose theta(t).
Track A inspected downgraded eid_dml_orthogonality_frontier_v1 and found a reviewer request for primitive Holder/entropy lower-bound witnesses, but it is a generic DML frontier rather than the Bonvini-Kennedy dose-response problem.
Track A inspected stat_sa_cate_pointwise_v1 and found reusable localized minimax machinery, but its target is CATE and was downgraded for target well-posedness.
Track A inspected pid_dose_response_shape_v1 and pid_dose_response_iv_v1; these are continuous-treatment partial-ID/shape entries, not Stat minimax lower-bound proposals.
No prior-proposal entry was cross-linked to the exact interior continuous-treatment dose-response minimax lower bound, so all surviving gaps are web-sourced.
